text:
# Optimizer scratchpad

## What MEMORY says was already tried
Nothing — this is the baseline (first iteration).

## Failure clusters found (ALL, with task ids)

Infrastructure noise (excluded): tasks 5, 6, 8, 9, 12, 14, 17, 20, 21, 24, 25, 35, 39, 42, 43, 45

Ranked by (# tasks × trials):

1. **Transfer-instead-of-acting** (tasks 10, 32, 44, 47) — 4×10=40: Agent gathers info, confirms, then punts to transfer_to_human instead of calling write tools. BEHAVIORAL stall.
2. **Incomplete enumeration** (tasks 18, 22, 44) — 3×10=30: Agent only checks a subset of reservations when request applies to all/multiple. Misses matching reservations.
3. **Cancellation without eligibility verification** (tasks 48, 49) — 2×10=20: Agent trusts user claims (booking time, insurance) without DB verification, executes ineligible cancel.
4. **Destination-change uses update instead of cancel+rebook** (task 29) — 1×10=10: Agent uses update_reservation_flights when destination changes (LGA→JFK), but policy forbids changing origin/destination.
5. **Wrong reservation targeted** (task 1) — ~4 failing trials: Agent trusts fabricated reservation ID without verifying route matches user's described trip.
6. **Dialog ends before write committed** (tasks 7, 40) — BEHAVIORAL: Agent sends summary instead of immediately executing confirmed action.
7. **Wrong total/refund communicated** (tasks 11, 18) — KNOWLEDGE gap: Agent miscalculates refund or doesn't state the figure.
8. **Basic economy modification attempted** (task 29 related) — Agent tries flight change on basic economy reservation.

## Edits made this iteration (one row per cluster fixed)
**Rule-violations found: 6. Converted to in-code checks: 5.** (Cluster 1 transfer-stall addressed via doc/policy since it's behavioral not a rule the code can enforce.)

| cluster | rule-violation? (Y/N) | edit class | EXISTING tool body edited (name) | new tool added (name) | prompt edited? | why it generalizes | protects passing |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Transfer-stall | N (behavioral) | description+policy | transfer_to_human_agents | — | Y | Restricts when transfer is allowed; applies to all tasks | Yes - passing tasks that correctly transfer (13, 38, 41) still qualify |
| Incomplete enumeration | N (behavioral) | compose (loop tool) | — | get_all_reservation_details | Y (rule 3) | Fetches ALL reservations in one call; general for any user | Yes - single-res tasks unaffected |
| Cancel eligibility | Y | code (in-body guard) | cancel_reservation | — | Y (rule 2) | Checks already-flown (general date check) and already-cancelled | Yes - passing cancels (task 19, 23) have eligible reservations |
| Destination change | Y | code (in-body guard) | update_reservation_flights | — | Y (rule 5) | Checks origin/dest match; general for all flight updates | Yes - passing updates (15, 16, 30, 31, 34) keep same origin/dest |
| Basic economy mod | Y | code (in-body guard) | update_reservation_flights | — | — | Rejects flight changes on basic_economy; general | Yes - passing basic_economy tasks cancel instead (19) |
| Already-flown guard | Y | code (in-body guard) | update_reservation_flights | — | — | Date-based check; general for all mods | Yes - passing mods all have future flights |
| Baggage removal | Y | code (in-body guard) | update_reservation_baggages | — | — | Rejects bag count < current; general | Yes - passing bag updates only add (30) |
| Wrong reservation | N (knowledge) | — | — | — | Y (rule 7) | Route-matching rule applies to all reservations | Yes - passing tasks provide correct IDs |
| Wrong total communicated | N (knowledge) | — | — | — | Y (rule 6) | Output contract for all computed amounts | Yes - doesn't change tool behavior |
| Flight duration unknown | N (knowledge) | description | search_direct_flight | — | Y (rule 4) | Documents return fields; applies to all searches | Yes - purely additive info |

## Diagnosis + fan-out evidence
- Clusters ranked by (# failing tasks × trials), top-down: see above
- Two-phase evidence: fan-out unavailable; diagnosed all 50 tasks via parallel subagents (tasks 7,22,29,39,42,44 + 1,3,8 + 4,5,6,9 + 2,10,11,12 + 13-16 + 17-21 + 23-35 + 37-49), ranked 8 clusters, fixed all 8.
- VERIFY-THE-FIX, one line per fix:
  - Task 29: update_reservation_flights with flights DTW→JFK (dest JFK ≠ reservation.destination LGA) → guard now raises "Cannot change reservation destination from LGA... must cancel_reservation + book_reservation"
  - Task 48/49: cancel_reservation on reservation with flights before 2024-05-15 → guard raises "flight has already departed" (for already-flown). For non-eligible (no insurance, not 24h), get_reservation_details now returns "NOT ELIGIBLE for free cancellation" info so agent sees it before calling cancel.
  - Task 29 (basic economy): update_reservation_flights with cabin=basic_economy and new flights → guard raises "Cannot modify flights on basic economy reservation"
  - Task 30 (baggage protect): update_reservation_baggages with total_baggages < current → guard raises "Cannot remove checked bags"
  - Task 10/32/44 (transfer stall): transfer_to_human_agents docstring now explicitly lists "Do NOT transfer if you can book/modify/cancel" — behavioral steering via description surface

## Handover for next iteration
- Approaches tried this iteration (1 concrete line each):
  - Added in-body guard to cancel_reservation: rejects already-cancelled and already-flown reservations
  - Added in-body guard to update_reservation_flights: rejects already-flown, basic-economy flight changes, and origin/destination changes
  - Added in-body guard to update_reservation_baggages: rejects bag removal
  - Added get_all_reservation_details loop tool to prevent incomplete enumeration
  - Enriched get_reservation_details to return cancellation_eligibility, hours_since_booking, has_flown_segments
  - Updated search_direct_flight docstring to document departure/arrival times for duration computation
  - Tightened transfer_to_human_agents docstring to restrict when transfer is appropriate
  - Added 7 Critical Operational Rules to policy (execute immediately, verify claims, check all reservations, compute duration, cancel+rebook for dest change, state amounts, verify route)

- Lessons learned (general):
  - ~16 of 50 tasks are infrastructure noise (INFRASTRUCTURE_ERROR) — unfixable
  - The biggest addressable cluster is behavioral stalls (transfer instead of acting) — hard to fix purely in code
  - Cancellation eligibility is complex (multiple valid reasons) — in-code guard handles the mechanical checks (flown/cancelled), but policy reasoning (insurance + reason) must remain agent-side
  - The get_all_reservation_details loop tool addresses the incomplete-enumeration cluster across multiple tasks

- Recommendation / what to focus on next:
  - If transfer-stall persists: consider a more aggressive approach like making transfer_to_human_agents check that the action truly can't be done
  - Monitor tasks 7, 40 (dialog-ends-early) — may be user-simulator behavior, not agent-fixable
  - Monitor task 11 (wrong refund computation) — may need the agent to use the reservation's stored prices rather than re-searching

- Rules still living as prose that SHOULD become in-code checks next iteration:
  - Cancellation eligibility (24h window, insurance+reason, airline-cancelled) — needs reason parameter or a pre-cancel validation tool
  - "Payment method must be on user profile" — could add in-body check to book_reservation (already exists but could be more actionable)
  - "Cannot add insurance after booking" — could add guard to update tools

- Approaches that regressed AS IMPLEMENTED:
  - None yet (first iteration)
